data.title: Targa Resources Q4 Net Income Rises
data.description:
WASHINGTON (dpa-AFX) - Targa Resources Corp. (TRGP) reported fourth quarter net income attributable to Targa Resources of $545 million compared to $351 million, a year ago. Adjusted EBITDA was $1.34 billion for the fourth quarter of 2025 compared to $1.12 billion, last year. Total revenues were $4.06 billion, compared to $4.41 billion, last year.

For 2026, Targa estimates full year adjusted EBITDA to be between $5.4 billion and $5.6 billion. For the first quarter of 2026, the company intends to recommend to Board an increase to quarterly common dividend to $1.25 per common share or $5.00 per common share annualized.

In pre-market trading on NYSE, Targa shares are down 0.23 percent to $227.09.

data.title: Russia's oil and gas revenue seen halving y/y in February
data.description:
Key points:
  • Russia's February oil and gas revenue seen at 410 bln roubles
  • Decline driven by a stronger rouble and lower oil prices
  • Monthly revenue may rise from January due to damper payment

Russian state oil and gas revenue is seen almost halving in February to 410 billion roubles ($5.35 billion) from the same month in 2025 due to a stronger local currency and lower oil prices, Reuters calculations showed on Thursday.

Oil and gas revenue is the main source of income for the Kremlin, accounting for more than a fifth of federal budget proceeds that have been drained by heavy defence and security spending since Russia began its military campaign in Ukraine in February 2022.

Reuters calculations are based on oil and gas production data, refining, and supplies on domestic and international markets.

Monthly revenue is expected to increase by 3.1% from January thanks to a subsidy, known as a damper payment, which is usually paid to the oil refineries.

However, the oil companies will this month make an expected 42 billion rouble damper payment to the state as fuel prices are making it less profitable to sell fuel abroad.

Russia's oil and gas revenue over January-February is expected to total 800 billion roubles, down from 1.56 trillion roubles in the first two months of 2025.

The budget forecasts income of 8.92 trillion roubles from oil and gas sales this year. Total budget revenue for 2026 is seen at 40.283 trillion roubles.

Last year, Russia's federal budget revenue from oil and gas dropped 24% to 8.48 trillion roubles, the lowest level since 2020.

($1 = 76.6500 roubles)

data.title: Appian Q4 revenue rises 22% on cloud subscriptions strength
data.description:
Overview

  • Process automation firm's Q4 revenue grew 22% yr/yr

  • GAAP net loss for Q4 was $5.1 mln, improving from last year

  • Company announced new AI capabilities and agreements with US Army and NZ Police

Outlook

  • Appian expects Q1 2026 cloud subscriptions revenue between $119.0 mln and $121.0 mln

  • Company forecasts full-year 2026 cloud subscriptions revenue between $502.0 mln and $510.0 mln

  • Appian anticipates Q1 2026 adjusted EBITDA between $19.0 mln and $22.0 mln

Result Drivers

  • CLOUD SUBSCRIPTIONS - Cloud subscriptions revenue rose 18% yr/yr to $117 mln, driving overall revenue growth

  • PROFESSIONAL SERVICES - Professional services revenue increased 36% yr/yr to $40.6 mln, contributing to total revenue growth

  • CLOUD ARR EXPANSION - Cloud net annualized recurring revenue expanded by 114% as of Dec 31, 2025, indicating strong demand

Key Details

Metric

Q4 Revenue

Beat/Miss

Actual

$202.9 mln

Consensus Estimate

$189 mln (6 Analysts)

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "hold" and the breakdown of recommendations is 2 "strong buy" or "buy", 5 "hold" and 1 "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the software peer group is "buy."

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Appian Corp is $38.00, about 57.9% above its February 18 closing price of $24.06

  • The stock recently traded at 31 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 59 three months ago

Press Release:

data.title: Upbound Group Q4 revenue rises
data.description:
Overview

  • Financial solutions provider's Q4 revenue grew 11% yr/yr, beats analyst expectations

  • Company expects 2026 revenue between $4.7 and $4.95 bln

  • Adjusted EBITDA for 2026 forecasted between $500 and $535 mln

Outlook

  • Company expects 2026 revenue between $4.7 bln and $4.95 bln

  • Upbound forecasts 2026 adjusted EBITDA of $500 mln to $535 mln

  • Company projects 2026 non-GAAP EPS between $4.00 and $4.35

Result Drivers

  • ACIMA PERFORMANCE - Acima reported its ninth consecutive quarter of GMV and revenue growth, with a 9% increase in revenue year-over-year

  • BRIGIT EXPANSION - Brigit generated $65 mln in revenue, supported by 1.6 mln paying subscribers and nearly 10% ARPU growth

data.title: Wall Street Cheers Nvidia, Meta AI Tie-Up Ahead of Earnings
data.description:
Shares of Nvidia were in focus after Stifel reiterated a bullish view, pointing to an expanded partnership with Meta Platforms tied to artificial intelligence infrastructure.

Stifel analyst Ruben Roy kept a Buy rating and a $250 price target on Nvidia, saying the deeper collaboration with Meta supports the company's long-term AI growth narrative.

The agreement covers a multi-year effort to build on-premises, cloud and AI systems. It includes broad use of Nvidia's CPUs, GPUs and Spectrum-X Ethernet networking, extending an existing relationship between the two companies.

One element highlighted by Stifel is Meta's plan to use Nvidia's rack-scale confidential computing for private processing within WhatsApp. Roy said this suggests Meta is comfortable relying on Nvidia's integrated approach across hardware and software.

The deal also marks an early large-scale deployment of Nvidia's Grace CPU-only systems. Both companies plan to co-design infrastructure and optimize software, signaling closer technical cooperation.

According to Roy, the adoption of Meta will increase the AI-centric networking products of Nvidia. Although large cloud providers are still working on creating proprietary chips, Stifel believes that Nvidia will remain competitive owing to the complete beat provided by its full-stack platform that encompasses silicon, systems, networking, and software.

data.title: INSM: 2025 revenues hit $606.4M, with strong ARIKAYCE growth and BRINSUPRI's U.S. launch outperforming
data.description:
Full-year 2025 revenues reached $606.4 million, with ARIKAYCE up 19% year-over-year and BRINSUPRI's U.S. launch exceeding expectations. 2026 guidance projects at least $1 billion in BRINSUPRI revenues and $450–$470 million for ARIKAYCE, with continued investment in pipeline and commercialization.

Original document: Insmed Incorporated [INSM] SEC 8-K Current Report — Feb. 19 2026

data.title: MD: 2025 net income rebounded to $165.4M on $1.91B revenue, with improved margins and strong cash flow
data.description:
Net revenue declined 4.9% to $1.91 billion in 2025 due to practice exits, but net income rebounded to $165.4 million from a prior year loss, with improved operating margins and strong cash flow. The company focused on core hospital-based services, completed strategic acquisitions, and repurchased $83.8 million in shares. Operational and regulatory risks remain significant.

Original document: Pediatrix Medical Group, Inc. [MD] SEC 10-K Annual Report — Feb. 19 2026

Disclaimer
This is an AI-generated summary and may contain inaccuracies. Please verify any important information with the original source.

data.title: BAND: 2025 saw double-digit revenue growth and margin gains, with 2026 guidance signaling further acceleration
data.description:
Record 2025 results featured 10% revenue growth, 19% cloud communications growth, and margin expansion, driven by AI voice adoption and software services. 2026 guidance projects 16% revenue growth, 20% EBITDA margin, and continued strong cash flow.

Original document: Bandwidth Inc. [BAND] Slides Release — Feb. 19 2026

Disclaimer

3.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None
